_comment: All 16 type write-ups. Codes = {A,D}{G,R}{S,P}{C,I}. Do not change codes or field names — they are used by the scoring engine and result page.

AGSC.name: The Anchored Shepherd
AGSC.identity: Your house runs toward you, not away from you.
AGSC.strength: You lead from conviction, not approval.
AGSC.strengthDetail: You know who you are in front of Allah and that clarity shapes how you lead your home and your brothers. When things are hard you do not wait for someone else to set the direction — you already know the direction. That kind of man is rare, and the people around you feel it even when they do not say it.
AGSC.blindSpot: You mistake silence for agreement from those you love.
AGSC.blindSpotDetail: Because you lead well, the people around you may stop telling you what is actually going on. Your wife may agree to keep the peace. Your children may not push back. Over time you can start to think everything is fine when there is something quietly building underneath. The strongest men need to create the most space for honest feedback — not less.
AGSC.quranAnchor: And We made them leaders, guiding by Our command. And We inspired them to do good deeds, establish prayer, and give zakah. And they were worshippers of Us.
AGSC.nextStep: Ask your wife one hard question this week — and sit with the answer without defending yourself.

AGSI.name: The Fortified Island
AGSI.identity: You are strong inside. The question is whether anyone else can see it.
AGSI.strength: You govern yourself and you lead your household with real intention.
AGSI.strengthDetail: Most men can do one of these. You are doing both. Your identity is grounded, your nafs is mostly in check, and your home has a sense of direction. That combination is built on years of quiet effort and it does not happen by accident. Allah has given you something most men are still searching for.
AGSI.blindSpot: You carry your strength alone, and that is starting to cost you.
AGSI.blindSpotDetail: A man who governs himself and leads his house without brothers around him will eventually make avoidable mistakes or burn out in ways he does not see coming. Brotherhood is not a luxury for men at your level — it is protection. The Prophet, peace be upon him, did not lead alone. Every strong man needs men who will tell him what he cannot see about himself.
AGSI.quranAnchor: And hold firmly to the rope of Allah all together and do not become divided.
AGSI.nextStep: Reach out to one man this week — not because you need something, but because isolation at your level is a risk you cannot afford.

AGPC.name: The Governed Brother
AGPC.identity: The men around you know your strength. Your home is still waiting for it.
AGPC.strength: You are anchored in who you are and you invest in other men.
AGPC.strengthDetail: You have done the inner work that most men avoid. You know what you stand for, you keep yourself in check, and you show up for your brothers in ways that matter. That combination builds trust and influence in your circle, and it does not happen by accident — it reflects real commitment to something beyond yourself.
AGPC.blindSpot: You lead everywhere except inside your own walls.
AGPC.blindSpotDetail: The hard conversations at home get put off. The tension gets managed instead of addressed. You are more present as a brother to other men than as a shepherd to your own wife and children. This is not about effort — you have plenty of that. It is about where you aim it. The men you lead outside need you to lead at home first, because that is where the real test is.
AGPC.quranAnchor: O you who believe, protect yourselves and your families from a fire whose fuel is people and stones.
AGPC.nextStep: Name one hard conversation at home you have been avoiding. Have it this week.

AGPI.name: The Disciplined Hermit
AGPI.identity: You run a clean internal life. The outside has not caught up yet.
AGPI.strength: Your self-discipline and sense of identity are genuinely strong.
AGPI.strengthDetail: You keep your promises to yourself. You know what you believe. Your personal practice is consistent where most men are chaotic. That inner foundation is real and it is the hardest thing to build — many men spend their whole lives chasing it. You have it. The question now is what you will build on top of it.
AGPI.blindSpot: Your strength has become a wall between you and the people who need you.
AGPI.blindSpotDetail: Self-sufficiency is a good quality that has gone too far. You handle things alone, avoid leading at home when it requires conflict, and have no brothers close enough to tell you what you cannot see. The man who needs no one eventually leads no one. Your discipline deserves a larger arena than yourself.
AGPI.quranAnchor: Indeed, Allah will not change the condition of a people until they change what is in themselves.
AGPI.nextStep: This week, choose one: step into a hard conversation at home, or reach out to one brother for a real conversation — not small talk.

ARSC.name: The Devoted Father
ARSC.identity: You are there for your family and your brothers. Your nafs is still in the fight with you.
ARSC.strength: You lead your home and you are present for the men around you.
ARSC.strengthDetail: Your family has a shepherd and your brothers have a brother. That is more than most men can say. You show up when it is inconvenient, you lead your house with intention, and your presence is felt. These are not small things — they are the marks of a man who has made real choices about what matters.
ARSC.blindSpot: Your reactive nafs is quietly undermining the leadership you are capable of.
ARSC.blindSpotDetail: Anger that comes out before you meant it. Promises to yourself that keep slipping. A phone that runs your evenings more than you do. These small breaks in self-governance add up. Your home and your brothers see a strong man — but you know there is a version of you that is more consistent, more patient, and less ruled by what you feel in the moment. That version is close.
ARSC.quranAnchor: And be patient, for indeed Allah does not allow to be lost the reward of those who do good.
ARSC.nextStep: Name your biggest recurring nafs battle out loud — then tell one brother about it this week.

ARSI.name: The Lone Shepherd
ARSI.identity: You lead your family with clear purpose. You are fighting two battles alone.
ARSI.strength: You know who you are and you lead your household.
ARSI.strengthDetail: Your identity is grounded and your home has direction. You do not wait for someone else to set the tone — you carry that responsibility yourself. That clarity and that willingness to lead are real strengths that your family depends on, even when they do not say it out loud.
ARSI.blindSpot: You are reactive and isolated, and the two together are a quiet danger.
ARSI.blindSpotDetail: Your nafs still gets the better of you more often than you want, and you are carrying that struggle completely alone. No brothers who know the real situation, no one to hold you accountable, no one to tell you what you look like from the outside. A man leading his family without brotherhood around him is one hard season away from a crisis he did not see coming.
ARSI.quranAnchor: The believer to the believer is like a building — each part strengthens the other.
ARSI.nextStep: Find one man you trust and tell him one real thing about what you are actually going through. Not a summary — the real thing.

ARPC.name: The Purposeful Brother
ARPC.identity: You know who you are and you have real men around you. Now lead from that foundation.
ARPC.strength: Your identity is clear and your brotherhood is genuine.
ARPC.strengthDetail: You know what you stand for and you have invested in real relationships with other men. That combination is rarer than you might think. Most men have one or the other — you have both. The foundation is there. The men around you already see something in you worth building with.
ARPC.blindSpot: Your nafs runs you at home in ways your brothers never see.
ARPC.blindSpotDetail: You lead well in your circle but avoid the hard leadership at home — the difficult conversations, the consistent presence your wife and children need. At the same time your nafs still gets ahead of your intentions in ways that slowly erode what you are trying to build. The gap between the man your brothers see and the man your family experiences is where the real work is.
ARPC.quranAnchor: O you who believe, fear Allah and be with the truthful.
ARPC.nextStep: Ask one of your brothers to hold you accountable on one specific thing at home this month.

ARPI.name: The Man With Purpose
ARPI.identity: You know who you are. Everything else is still being built.
ARPI.strength: Your identity is the one solid leg of your result — and it is enough to build from.
ARPI.strengthDetail: Knowing who you are and what you stand for is the hardest thing to establish. Many men live their whole lives without answering that question clearly. You have answered it. That is not a small thing. It means you have a foundation. The question now is whether you will build the life on top of it that your purpose demands.
ARPI.blindSpot: Purpose without structure and brotherhood is potential that never lands.
ARPI.blindSpotDetail: You know the direction but your nafs keeps pulling you off course, you are not leading your home with the consistency your family needs, and you are carrying all of this alone. Each of those three things weakens the others. A man with your level of clarity who builds the right habits and finds the right brothers becomes something different entirely.
ARPI.quranAnchor: Indeed, Allah will not change the condition of a people until they change what is in themselves.
ARPI.nextStep: Pick one: govern your nafs for 30 days on one specific battle, or take one real step toward leading at home. Do not try both at once.

DGSC.name: The Steady Provider
DGSC.identity: You show up consistently for your family and your brothers. Now answer the question of why.
DGSC.strength: You are dependable and your people trust your presence.
DGSC.strengthDetail: Your family has a provider who shows up. Your brothers have a man who is there. Your self-discipline keeps the daily structure intact. These are not defaults — they are choices you have made and kept. The people in your life have real security because of how you move, even when you do not feel like it.
DGSC.blindSpot: You lead others without a clear internal compass, and that will catch up with you.
DGSC.blindSpotDetail: Dependability without direction is a man who runs well but does not know where he is going. When your children ask you what you are building your life toward, what do you say? When your wife wonders whether you have a vision for your family, what does she see? The men you are close to can feel that something is missing even if they cannot name it. Clarity of purpose is what turns a disciplined man into a leading man.
DGSC.quranAnchor: And upon Allah let the believers rely.
DGSC.nextStep: Write one sentence this week: what do you believe Allah put you specifically on this earth to do? Keep it and revisit it.

DGSI.name: The Quiet Shepherd
DGSI.identity: You carry your family with discipline. Your identity is still forming and you are doing it alone.
DGSI.strength: You govern yourself and you lead your household with real care.
DGSI.strengthDetail: Two of the four axes are genuinely strong. You keep yourself in check and your home has direction. That combination means your family is protected and your own life has structure. Most men get neither. The fact that you have both tells you something real about your character.
DGSI.blindSpot: You do not know yet why you are doing all of it, and you are not connected to men who could help you figure that out.
DGSI.blindSpotDetail: Leading a home and governing a nafs without a clear sense of purpose is like running a disciplined household with no destination in mind. It works until it does not. And doing it without brotherhood means there is no one to say, brother, this is where I see you drifting. Those two gaps together are what stop a good man from becoming a great one.
DGSI.quranAnchor: Whoever fears Allah, He will make for him a way out and provide for him from where he does not expect.
DGSI.nextStep: Go to Jumu'ah this week and stay after. Introduce yourself to one man and stay in contact.

DGPC.name: The Brotherhood Man
DGPC.identity: Your circle is strong and your personal life is in order. Now bring that home.
DGPC.strength: You invest in men around you and you keep yourself in check.
DGPC.strengthDetail: The combination of real brotherhood and personal discipline is rare. Most men have social connections that go nowhere or self-discipline that is disconnected from community. You have both. Your brothers trust you and your internal life is ordered. That is a solid foundation.
DGPC.blindSpot: Your home does not get what your brotherhood gets, and you are not fully anchored in your why.
DGPC.blindSpotDetail: You are more consistent for the men around you than for the people inside your house. The hard conversations at home are getting avoided. The direction your family needs from you is not yet clear. And underneath all of it there is a question of purpose — why you are doing this, what it is building toward — that has not been fully answered. Brotherhood without that anchor eventually loses its depth too.
DGPC.quranAnchor: And cooperate in righteousness and piety, and do not cooperate in sin and transgression.
DGPC.nextStep: Bring one thing you practice with your brothers — honest conversation, accountability, reflection — into your relationship with your wife this week.

DGPI.name: The Disciplined Ghost
DGPI.identity: You carry your weight with discipline. Nobody else is carrying it with you.
DGPI.strength: Your self-governance is real and more developed than most men your age.
DGPI.strengthDetail: You keep promises to yourself, you hold your nafs in check, and your daily life has more structure than chaos. That is the hardest internal work to do and most men never get there. You have built that foundation quietly, often without anyone noticing or affirming it. It is real.
DGPI.blindSpot: Discipline without direction, home leadership, or brotherhood is a man who is prepared for a journey he has not started.
DGPI.blindSpotDetail: You are governing a life that has not yet found its purpose. You are leading a nafs that has no clear mission to point at. You are doing it all alone with no men who know what you are actually carrying. The good news is that the hardest thing — self-governance — is already in place. What is missing is the vision and the community to point all of that discipline toward something real.
DGPI.quranAnchor: And remind, for indeed the reminder benefits the believers.
DGPI.nextStep: Go to Jumu'ah this week and stay after. One real conversation with one man. That is the first step.

DRSC.name: The Present Man
DRSC.identity: You are there for your family and your brothers. Now anchor yourself in what you are actually leading toward.
DRSC.strength: Your people feel your presence and they lean on you.
DRSC.strengthDetail: You show up for your family and your brothers in ways that matter to them. That relational presence is something many disciplined men never develop. People feel safe around you, they call on you, they lean on you in hard moments. That is a form of leadership that is not to be taken lightly.
DRSC.blindSpot: Presence without direction and self-governance leaves your family without a map.
DRSC.blindSpotDetail: You are in the room but not always sure where the room is heading. Your nafs still runs you in ways that quietly undermine your leadership. And underneath all of it there is a question of purpose — what exactly are you building, and why — that has not been answered with clarity. Your family and brothers follow your presence. They need your direction too.
DRSC.quranAnchor: Men are the caretakers of women because Allah has given some of them an advantage over others and because they spend from their wealth.
DRSC.nextStep: Name out loud one direction you want to take your family in the next year. Say it to your wife this week.

DRSI.name: The Struggling Shepherd
DRSI.identity: You are showing up for your family. Three of the four pillars still need building.
DRSI.strength: You lead your household and your family has a shepherd.
DRSI.strengthDetail: You are showing up for your family even when it is hard. That is not nothing — there are men who do not. Your children and your wife have someone who is trying to lead them with intention, and that effort is real even when it is inconsistent. Do not dismiss that.
DRSI.blindSpot: You are leading without a foundation — no clear purpose, no governed nafs, and no brothers.
DRSI.blindSpotDetail: Leading your home is the one strong axis you have. But a shepherd without a compass, without self-governance, and without other shepherds around him will eventually lose his way. The season you are in now is not permanent — but the three gaps together mean that a hard month could knock over what you have been building. The work is urgent and it is specific.
DRSI.quranAnchor: So race to all that is good. Wherever you may be, Allah will bring you all together.
DRSI.nextStep: Pick the one gap — purpose, nafs, or brotherhood — that if addressed first would make the other two easier. Start there this week.

DRPC.name: The Social Drifter
DRPC.identity: You have real brothers around you. That is the seed. Now grow the rest.
DRPC.strength: You are not isolated — you have men around you and that matters more than you know.
DRPC.strengthDetail: Brotherhood is the one strong axis in your result. That means you are not doing this alone, even if it does not feel that way yet. You have access to men who can help you build the things that are missing. Most men who drift do so in complete isolation. You have a resource that others do not — use it.
DRPC.blindSpot: Surface community is not the same as real brotherhood, and neither is a substitute for purpose and self-governance.
DRPC.blindSpotDetail: You may be around men without being truly accountable to them. You may be connected without being honest about what is actually happening in your life. And the bigger gaps — a clear purpose, a governed nafs, and real leadership at home — cannot be filled by community alone. The brotherhood around you is a door. But you have to walk through it with honesty, not just presence.
DRPC.quranAnchor: O you who believe, fear Allah and be with the truthful.
DRPC.nextStep: Have one real conversation with one of your brothers this week — not small talk. Tell him one true thing about where you are struggling.

DRPI.name: The Drifter
DRPI.identity: You are going through the motions. The fact that you took this tells you something in you wants more.
DRPI.strength: You were honest enough to answer these questions — and that honesty is where everything starts.
DRPI.strengthDetail: Most men in your position do not take an assessment like this. They stay busy, they manage the surface, they tell themselves it is fine. You did something different. You sat with these questions and answered them honestly. That requires a kind of self-awareness that is the first ingredient of real change. Do not underestimate what it means that you are here.
DRPI.blindSpot: You are managing life, not leading it — and all four areas need attention.
DRPI.blindSpotDetail: Your identity is unclear, your nafs is running you, your home does not have your full leadership, and you are carrying it all alone. That is a hard combination to hear. But it is also a clear picture — and a clear picture means a clear starting point. The path forward does not require fixing everything at once. It requires starting with the one thing that matters most right now and not stopping.
DRPI.quranAnchor: Has the time not come for those who have believed that their hearts should become humbly submissive at the remembrance of Allah?
DRPI.nextStep: Pray Fajr on time tomorrow. Not a plan for the week — just tomorrow. Then the day after. Build from there.
